events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario is an unprotected trench ≥5 ft with workers in it, work in-progress. This is a critical OSHA violation. Decision: fail. Risk: critical. Component conditions: non-compliant. Findings and actions straightforward.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 40,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.98,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Unprotected trench 5 ft or deeper with workers inside, creating imminent cave-in/entrapment hazard",
    "No protective system (sloping, shoring, shielding, or trench box) in place as required for excavations 5 ft or greater",
    "No apparent competent person inspection of excavation and protective system before entry"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately remove all workers from the trench",
    "Do not allow re-entry until an adequate protective system is installed (trench box, shoring, sloping/benching per soil type)",
    "Have a competent person inspect the excavation, classify the soil, and select/approve the protective system",
    "Provide safe access/egress (ladder, ramp, or steps) within 25 ft of every worker once protection is in place",
    "Keep spoil piles, equipment, and materials at least 2 ft back from the edge"
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1926 Subpart P requires a protective system for any excavation 5 ft or deeper unless the excavation is in stable rock. Workers in an unprotected trench of this depth are exposed to a high probability of fatal cave-in, making this a critical, stop-work condition.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1926.652 - Requirements for protective systems",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1926.651 - Specific excavation requirements (access/egress, spoil placement, competent person)"
  ]
}
